title: riddellia gnaphalioides
intro: Riddellia gnaphalioides, also known as the Navajo tea plant, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Riddellia gnaphalioides can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
